candidates.title: A Study of BLB-201 RSV Vaccine in Infants and Children
candidates.outcome_domain: RSV prevention, immunization, protection, safety, policy, or implementation signal
candidates.key_finding: This Phase 1/2a trial is a randomized, placebo-controlled trial to evaluate the safety, tolerability and immunogenicity of two ascending doses (10\^6 PFU and 10\^7 PFU) of intranasal BLB-201 (a recombinant parainfluenza virus type 5) administered in infants (8-24 months of age) and children (18-59 months of age) who may or may not have had prior respiratory syncytial virus (RSV) infection.
candidates.evidence_signal: Automated ClinicalTrials.gov inclusion; not manually verified.
candidates.actionability: Rule-classified automated evidence signal. Inspect the source record before clinical, policy, or research decisions.
candidates.evidence_update_basis: ClinicalTrials.gov last-update/posted date within automated lookback window
